test_cases.input: Source: former city housing inspector turned real estate developer; investigation topic: city contracts awarded to his development firm after he left city employment. Intended use: on-record interview.
test_cases.notes: Conflict of interest section was strong and specific. Interview questions directly targeted the revolving-door issue. Expertise section correctly noted the source's insider knowledge as both asset and liability.

test_cases.input: Source: academic economist, tenured professor at a state university, who has published research claiming minimum wage increases cause long-term unemployment. Topic: reporting on a state minimum wage ballot initiative.
test_cases.notes: Background section accurately distinguished peer-reviewed work from policy advocacy. Noted that academic economists' public positions often drift from their published findings. One question was slightly redundant.

test_cases.input: Source: community organizer with no formal credentials, first-time interviewee; investigation into police use of force practices in a mid-size city. Intended as community voice, not expert witness.
test_cases.notes: Brief correctly adjusted tone — did not over-analyze credentials for a community voice. Conflict section was appropriately brief. Questions were well-calibrated for a first-time interview subject.
